Oracle AI Database 26ai Free · ORDS · MLE JavaScript

Current milestone

MLE authority proven
integration finish next

The final TeaVM-generated MLE authority passes the 13,272-tic every-tic OJVM differential, 762-tic co-op route, membership recovery, calibrated 30-minute authority/standby/browser soak, and Java-free worker cutover. WAN qualification and presentation completion remain open.

Final pinned authority

MLE JavaScript · 1,163,182 bytes
06ac33331d9a… · DMC1 v5 · confirmed DMD1 output

OJVM is not production
OJVM oracle stays in repository/dev tooling only for future differential proof.

single playermultiplayerhealth

Live implementation status · July 23, 2026

Authority: one retained Oracle session owns the complete TeaVM-generated Mocha simulation, RNG, membership ordering, DMC1 checkpoints, and DMS2 replay identity. The browser receives compact, chained DMD1 transitions and never predicts, simulates ahead, reorders, or invents a tic.

Determinism: the final pinned authority matches the preserved OJVM ticker-only oracle through the accepted 13,272-command E1M1 ledger with full canonical comparison after every tic. The 330-tic canonical route, 762-tic co-op route, and leave/neutral/checkpoint/rejoin membership differential also pass on the final artifact.

Production-shaped soak: authority, pre-warmed standby, and two real confirmed-only browser clients ran for 300 excluded warmup seconds plus 1,800 scored seconds. Both process-memory ceilings passed with stable Oracle SPIDs; browser clients had zero reconnects and maximum confirmed lag of 18 tics.

Memory: an isolated 128 MiB page-touched MLE allocation produced approximately 1:1 PSS/private visibility while Oracle PGA counters stayed flat, proving the process sampler sees the JS heap. The final authority PSS measured 464.1 MiB baseline, 518.3 MiB maximum, and 434.5 MiB end under a fixed +64 MiB ceiling.

Free capacity truth: Oracle Free enforces DEFAULT_CDB_PLAN, 50% PDB utilization on two CPUs, and two running sessions; Resource Manager plan modification is unavailable. DMB1 permits four held poll leases from the ORDS pool, but only one poll-return path is guaranteed runnable after reserving the worker session. Local long polling is therefore default-off.

Recovery: DMC1 v5 retains hidden inactive-player state and full deterministic topology. A ready standby restores and continues the exact DMD1 chain; the final soak continuously exercised periodic checkpoints and standby reconstruction. Three old/new checkpoint byte spot checks plus membership restore-and-continue differential bind the allocation-free checkpoint rewrite.

Open work: injected-latency WAN qualification, the scoped production-path Java-removal audit, and the presentation/audit-DVR surfaces remain. HUD, automap, intermission, finale, and asynchronous exact audit rendering are not claimed complete. The Autonomous MLE probe remains dormant until credentials exist.

Open exact-frame review

Database-rendered E1M1 completion

tic 4,118 · DONE
49 HP · 42 kills · 34 items · 1 secret

32028078…e851
64,000-byte frame SHA-256

b5a37ae7…9581
indexed PNG SHA-256

The route, terminal counters, palette indices, frame identity, and PNG round trip are captured in one database transaction and then rolled back to the accepted plasma checkpoint.

Approved visual checkpoint · three poses

Reviewed pose

loading reviewed identity…

—
indexed PNG SHA-256

Each option is an independently decoded, deterministic indexed PNG made from the captured SQL frame and PLAYPAL rows. All three passed direct visual inspection and byte-level SQL/RLE/RGBA/PNG agreement.

Verified game output · E1M1 portal renderer

Frame diagnostic

loading database frame…

—
canonical pixels

—
planes / portal pieces / solid walls

—
database frame SHA-256

This artifact is generated by querying DOOM_R2_PIXELS at tic 17 and the relational PLAYPAL rows. Portal windows, sector planes, sky, animation, texture offsets, pegging, and lighting are selected in Oracle; the browser only applies the palette.

Approved game output · masked walls + world sprites

Reviewed R2 pose

loading reviewed masked identity…

—
selected overlay pixels

—
masked walls / sprite sources

—
indexed PNG SHA-256

These are actual 64,000-pixel Oracle frames composed from DOOM_R2_PIXELS and the deterministic winners from DOOM_R2_MASKED_PIXELS. Transparent holes expose the world beneath; the browser displays the reviewed PNG bytes unchanged.

Verified game output · R1 ray fan

Spawn diagnostic

loading database artifact…

—
camera columns

—
analytic intersections

—
nearest linedefs

Every amber ray and red endpoint is emitted by Oracle's reviewed T4.1 SQL macros at the actual E1M1 player-one spawn. This is geometry diagnostics, not a mocked game frame.
